discussion_comments.comment_body:
Is there any particular reason why MINUS has been moved one line higher apart from keeping all operators together? My assumption is that minus was recognized as another token.

I am afraid this might be a breaking change, since we are changing priority. Previously `-` was recognized as NODE_STRING, now it is recognized as minus instead.

I don't know how many people were using it and, perhaps, we could sacrifice it, but I've got to mention, that previously this was working, and no it is not (have a look at `b` node). This works fine in mermaid.live, but not in the branch:

```
flowchart TD

a[|key:value|Title1]
b[|key:-value|Title2]
```

we also allow dash as node name (without wrapping in quotes), but that is OK
```
flowchart TD

- --> 2
- --> 1
```
@nirname, isn't it possible to edit the grammar to support both usecase?

The feature idea is brilliant! Cudos to @Zumbala  but this is a blocker. 

Any existing diagrams with a minus in the label without quotes will suddenly break. I think there can be many of those. A minus sign is fairly common.
😞 

Let's try to modify the grammar to accommodate this!!! The idea is brilliant, and the implementation is good (apart from the blocker). 💯

discussion_comments.commented_code:
@@ -0,0 +1,46 @@
+grammar Sankey
+
+// common stuff in ../common/common.langium
+// can't import directly because of SANKEY_LINK_SOURCE parse rule
discussion_comments.comment_body: What's special with SANKEY_LINK_SOURCE that prevents the import?

discussion_comments.commented_code:
@@ -0,0 +1,46 @@
+grammar Sankey
+
+// common stuff in ../common/common.langium
+// can't import directly because of SANKEY_LINK_SOURCE parse rule
discussion_comments.comment_body:
When importing from another  `.langium`, the order of possible tokens will be the following:

```javascript
[
  WHITESPACE,
  NEWLINE,
  ...,
  SANKEY_LINK_SOURCE,
  ...,
  TITLE,
  ACC_DESCR,
  ACC_TITLE
]
```

So it will try tokenizing `SANKEY_LINK_SOURCE` before `TITLE` and other rules. But the `SANKEY_LINK_SOURCE` could match everything:

https://github.com/mermaid-js/mermaid/blob/b6906a6474e01b5a6946749700150a4bada61657/packages/parser/src/language/sankey/sankeyMatcher.ts#L4

So it won't tokenize `TITLE` because of that. That's why I unimported `common.langium` and added all rules in the diagram langium grammar file.

discussion_comments.commented_code:
@@ -0,0 +1,46 @@

+

+// can't import directly because of SANKEY_LINK_SOURCE parse rule

@@ -0,0 +1,46 @@
+grammar Sankey
+
+// common stuff in ../common/common.langium
+// can't import directly because of SANKEY_LINK_SOURCE parse rule
discussion_comments.comment_body:
I wouldn't recommend overriding `TokenBuilder` unless we explicitly need to. It would be better to keep rules in grammar file (`.langium` file).

> ...aren't part of grammar (`.langium` file); they're implemented in `TokenBuilder`, which would diverge and make it more complicated to adjust more services for straight-forward grammar.
>
> https://github.com/mermaid-js/mermaid/pull/4799#discussion_r1329939377

Creating a complex custom pattern function just to check that all other possible rules aren't applied is kinda odd.

---

Another possible solution is to manually reorder rules.
We could take that array of rules and change the index of `SANKEY_LINK_NODE` to the end, similar to `mindmap` diagram:

https://github.com/mermaid-js/mermaid/blob/1fa9dd7a1ba7b6ac229a8ecf9ce4d1068a6ec21c/packages/parser/src/language/mindmap/mindmapTokenBuilder.ts#L28-L34

discussion_comments.commented_code:
@@ -0,0 +1,46 @@
+grammar Sankey
+
+// common stuff in ../common/common.langium
+// can't import directly because of SANKEY_LINK_SOURCE parse rule
discussion_comments.comment_body:
I 100% agree that it'd be best to keep all possible rules in the grammar file. 
I'm not advocating otherwise.
I'm just trying to find a solution to the problem that langium does not provide a mechanism for specifying the order of imported rules with respect to the current grammar file. (Ex: the rules from `common.langium` are not ordered as one would expect -- before
the sankey grammar rules.)  

Having to manually reorder the rules (as in the mindmap example) is proof  that langium is lacking this fundamental capability.

The solution that I proposed and am exploring is not overriding `TokenBuilder` at all.  It's using the capabilities that Chevotain has.
There are many instances of when simple RegExps do not suffice; that's why Chevotain has included the ability to define custom functions.

All that said: I'm pretty disappointed that it all means that we cannot import common aspects of grammars.  
Grammars can be persnickity and having to continually edit each diagram's grammar separately to make the same single change is terrible. (As anyone that has worked on the existing jison grammars here knows.)

discussion_comments.commented_code:
@@ -0,0 +1,46 @@
+grammar Sankey
+
+// common stuff in ../common/common.langium
+// can't import directly because of SANKEY_LINK_SOURCE parse rule
discussion_comments.comment_body:
FYI - I have it working:
- in `sankey.langium` we can now `import "../common/common";` (no need to duplicate the code)
- in `sankeyTokenBuilder` I replaced `tokenType.PATTERN = regexPatternFunc(sankeyLinkNodeRegex);` with `tokenType.PATTERN = matchSankeyLinkNode;`
- matchSankeyLinkNode() ensures that the SANKEY_LINK_NODE does not inadvertently match the diagram title,  a11y title, or a11y description . I used the regexps already defined in ../common/matcher` (`accessibilityDescrRegex, accessibilityTitleRegex, titleRegex`)

I'll do a draft PR tomorrow with my code.

I find this much, much clearer and it will be _much_ more maintainable than re-ordering the tokens generated by the lexer.  It also means that we won't (continue to) have duplicated grammar code to maintain.

commented_code:
+grammar Timeline
+import "../common/common";
+
+entry Timeline:
+  NEWLINE*
+  TIMELINE_KEYWORD NEWLINE*
+  TitleAndAccessibilities?
+  sections+=TimelineSection* | periods+=TimelinePeriod*
+;
+
+TimelineSection:
+  title=TIMELINE_SECTION_TITLE NEWLINE*
+  (periods+=TimelinePeriod)*
+;
+
+TimelinePeriod:
+  title=TIMELINE_PERIOD_TITLE NEWLINE* (":" events+=(TIMELINE_EVENT | TIMELINE_PERIOD_TITLE) NEWLINE*)*
+;
+
+terminal TIMELINE_KEYWORD: /timeline(?!\S)/;
+terminal TIMELINE_SECTION_TITLE: /timeline-section/;
+// TODO: figue out how to prevent `PERIOD_TITLE` to consume `EVENT`

discussion_comments.comment_body:
@msujew Is there a better way to handle this? The context is that there are two terminal rule identical in pattern: `TIMELINE_PERIOD_TITLE` and `TIMELINE_EVENT`, but there is some differences of the return type of `ValueConverter` (replace `
` with `\n`). `TIMELINE_PERIOD_TITLE` occurs before `TIMELINE_EVENT`, so when I want to parse `TIMELINE_EVENT` it would be tokenized as `TIMELINE_PERIOD_TITLE`, and `ValueConverter` won't do changes as expected. example: ``` timeline period title: event
wow ```

discussion_comments.comment_body:
In current `jison` grammar, the `:` is part of rule itself:

<https://github.com/mermaid-js/mermaid/blob/38f2ff0bca4e0f816256d1fc11469e8502b16316/packages/mermaid/src/diagrams/timeline/parser/timeline.jison#L41>

Whereas in the current `langium` grammar isn't not:

<https://github.com/mermaid-js/mermaid/blob/822e4d30984cc6d4c1b4a51a1b2499e46719f20a/packages/parser/src/language/timeline/timeline.langium#L25>

Thanks for the highlight! Although not it won't make symbols as `operators` or `symbols` in generated `monarch`, but at least it's working as expected.

Isn't this removing the `ARROW_DIRECTION` feature then? Is that not being used?
Was it undocumented?

The` ARROW_DIRECTION `feature is still supported. The terminal was removed to fix parsing conflicts with IDs starting with L/R/T/B. Directions are now parsed as IDs and validated downstream(in DB layer)

I don't see any code related to DB in this PR 🤔 
Also, `rhsDir=ID ':'` now rhsDir is ID, so not a direction at all, so shouldn't we fix the jison as well?

> I don't see any code related to DB in this PR 🤔 Also, `rhsDir=ID ':'` now rhsDir is ID, so not a direction at all, so shouldn't we fix the jison as well?

The DB validations were already in place ,  `rhsDir` suggests it should be a direction, it's still semantically correct - it will be a direction after validation
